title: The Unnamed Snake Decides
tagline: The unnamed snake from EP031's postscript has been thinking about its name. It writes back. The team learns what it chose.
hook_line: Three weeks after Archie's letter — 'deciding carefully is not the same as not knowing' — a reply arrives from the eastern valley. The main letter is Medusa's. The postscripts are from the usual left-side correspondents. And at the very bottom, in handwriting that is new and slightly wobbly, as if the writer is still figuring out how to hold the quill, is a postscript with a signature.

scenes.title: The Reply Arrives
scenes.narration: Perseus brought the letter to the main hall without opening it. He had learned to do this — the eastern letters were read together when they arrived, because everyone wanted to be there for the postscripts. The team assembled. Medusa's main letter was about the spring light (still good), the Titan (it had replied to the documentation Hades sent and had asked if the underworld needed anything), and the second sculpture (she had added a small figure at the channel viewpoint, looking down at where the underground river ran). Then the left-side postscripts: SEVEN, THREE, FOUR, FIVE, TWO, ONE, in their usual styles. And then. At the bottom. A wobbly signature.
scenes.visual_prompt: The main hall assembled for letter-reading — Perseus presenting the unopened letter — the team gathered: Athena, Zeus, Poseidon, Hercules, Artemis, Archie on the windowsill — Perseus reading Medusa's letter: spring light, Titan replying to Hades's documentation, the sculpture addition (small figure at the viewpoint) — the left-side postscripts reading in familiar sequence: SEVEN's caps, THREE's brevity, FOUR's group note, FIVE's self-description, TWO's hello, ONE's monitoring update — and then the pause before the last postscript — Zeus's crown listing left in anticipation — the letter visible with all the postscripts visible, the final one at the very bottom — #FFD700 and main hall warmth, the letter as the central prop with everyone leaning in

scenes.title: The New Postscript
scenes.narration: Perseus read the final postscript aloud. The handwriting was wobbly — not from age, from newness. Someone who had just learned to hold the quill. It said: 'Hello. I have been thinking about what you wrote. About deciding carefully not being the same as not knowing. I decided. I am RIVER. Because the river was there the whole time even though it was underground. And I was there the whole time too. I am new to writing. This took me eleven tries. — RIVER.' Perseus read it twice. There was a quiet in the room. Then Poseidon said: 'That is an excellent name.' 'Yes,' Zeus said. His crown was straight. 'Tell RIVER we said so.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Perseus reading the RIVER postscript — the wobbly handwriting visible: clearly someone new to writing, the letters uneven, the quill strokes over-deliberate — the text: 'I am RIVER. Because the river was there the whole time even though it was underground. And I was there the whole time too. I am new to writing. This took me eleven tries.' — the room absorbing this — Poseidon: 'that is an excellent name' — Zeus: 'yes. Tell RIVER we said so.' — Zeus's crown straight for the full delivery of this — Archie on the windowsill, his golden eyes — his letter said 'deciding carefully is not the same as not knowing' — RIVER decided — the postscript as the episode's emotional center, the wobbly handwriting the key visual — #FFD700 and the quiet of the room after

scenes.title: Why the River
scenes.narration: Athena had been still since Perseus read the name. She said: 'RIVER chose that name because we found the river.' 'Yes,' Perseus said. 'We went looking for it after the Titan mentioned it.' 'And Poseidon found it underground.' 'Yes.' 'So RIVER chose a name based on something that happened because the Titan remembered something and we went looking.' She looked at Archie. 'And then you wrote to the unnamed snake about deciding carefully.' Archie did not slow-blink. He just looked at her. 'The name RIVER exists,' Athena said, 'because of a chain of things that each happened because of another thing.' She opened her scroll. 'I am going to write this down.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena tracing the chain: Titan mentioned the river → team went looking → Poseidon found it underground → Archie's letter about deciding carefully → RIVER chose the name because the river was there the whole time, underground — 'the name RIVER exists because of a chain of things that each happened because of another thing' — Archie not slow-blinking, just watching her think — Athena opening her scroll to write the chain down — the chain visible as she articulates it, each link: Titan's message, the expedition, Poseidon's hands on the ground, Archie's letter, RIVER's decision — Hercules looking at the chain as Athena traces it, arriving at the same weight she did — #FFD700 and Athena's thinking-quality, the chain as the central invisible structure of the scene

scenes.title: SEVEN Weighs In
scenes.narration: SEVEN's postscript — which Perseus went back to read — had an addendum he'd missed on first pass. At the bottom of SEVEN's message, in SEVEN's reliable caps: 'P.S. I HELPED RIVER WITH THE QUILL. ELEVEN TRIES IS ACCURATE. THE FIRST SEVEN WERE MY FAULT. — SEVEN.' Hercules said: 'SEVEN helped RIVER learn to write.' 'Yes,' Perseus said. 'And is taking partial responsibility for the first seven tries.' 'That is very honest,' Hercules said. 'SEVEN is always honest,' Perseus said. He said it like he knew. Artemis looked at him. 'You've been paying attention to which snake is which.' 'Yes,' Perseus said. 'Left side. SEVEN is honest. THREE is economical. FOUR makes group decisions. FIVE thinks it's skeptical but is curious. TWO says hello. ONE monitors.' Artemis wrote this in her notebook.
scenes.visual_prompt: Perseus finding SEVEN's addendum: 'I HELPED RIVER WITH THE QUILL. ELEVEN TRIES IS ACCURATE. THE FIRST SEVEN WERE MY FAULT.' — Hercules: 'SEVEN is taking partial responsibility' / 'that is very honest' / 'SEVEN is always honest' — Artemis looking at Perseus when he says it like he knows — Perseus's full character breakdown of the left-side snakes: SEVEN (honest), THREE (economical), FOUR (group decisions), FIVE (thinks skeptical, is curious), TWO (says hello), ONE (monitors), RIVER (new, names things for what they are) — Artemis writing all of this in her notebook — the specific moment of Artemis writing down Perseus's observations, which is what Artemis does when something is accurate — Archie on the sill, watching — #FFD700 and the letter with its addenda, SEVEN's caps the visual contrast against the wobbly RIVER handwriting

scenes.title: Poseidon's Reply to the Titan
scenes.narration: While the RIVER celebration continued, Poseidon worked on the reply to the detail from Medusa's letter: the Titan had asked if the underworld needed anything. Poseidon drafted the response — this required checking with Hades, which meant sending a message to the underworld, which was a process he did not take lightly (you did not send casual messages to Hades). His draft: 'Dear Titan — We passed your question to Hades. Hades says the underworld does not currently need anything, but that he was pleased to be asked. He says he will let you know if that changes. Poseidon.' He paused over his note. He added a sardine. He considered whether you sent sardines to the underworld. He sent it anyway.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon drafting the Titan-to-underworld relay — the procedural weight of messaging Hades: 'you do not send casual messages to Hades' — his draft: professional, clean, passing Hades's response accurately — and then the sardine question: does one send sardines to the underworld? — Poseidon adding the sardine anyway — the draft going out via dolphin to the Titan, the message eventually reaching Hades through channels that take longer to describe than to use — Poseidon's log open, this entry in it — the sardine on the note as the recurring certainty in an uncertain situation — Hercules watching Poseidon add the sardine and choosing not to ask — #FFD700 and Poseidon's desk, the note with the sardine pressed on it, the procedural seriousness

scenes.title: What Goes Back East
scenes.narration: The reply letter to the eastern valley assembled. Perseus's letter to Medusa (spring light reply, note about the figure she added to the sculpture viewpoint — he wanted to know who it was). SEVEN: congratulations on eleven tries, the first seven were formative. THREE: RIVER is a good name. (That was all THREE wrote.) FOUR: we approve. (FOUR always spoke for the group.) FIVE: I also took a long time to decide things. I am telling RIVER this because it might help. (FIVE was being very supportive.) TWO: congratulations RIVER. ONE: I have been monitoring RIVER's progress. It was going well. Archie's contribution, through Athena: 'RIVER: you did it.' And Zeus, on his own small paper: 'RIVER: from your left-side neighbors. We are glad you are RIVER.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The assembly of all replies — each one in its character voice: Perseus's specific questions, SEVEN's accountability, THREE's economy ('RIVER is a good name.' period), FOUR's group approval, FIVE's unexpected solidarity (took me long too), TWO's congratulations, ONE's monitoring report, Archie's through Athena ('RIVER: you did it.'), and Zeus on his small paper: 'RIVER: from your left-side neighbors. We are glad you are RIVER.' — the stack of replies, each one in different handwriting and length — Zeus's small paper the last one added — the envelope addressed east — Archie watching the assembly — Poseidon's separate envelope for the Titan going via dolphin — #FFD700 and the warmth of collective correspondence, the letter stack the key visual

scenes.title: Archie and the Feather Mark
scenes.narration: Athena sealed Archie's contribution — 'RIVER: you did it.' — with the feather mark she had started drawing when he contributed something. She paused before she sealed the full letter. She looked at Archie. She said: 'Your letter worked.' Archie slow-blinked. She looked at her scroll where she had written the chain — Titan, river, expedition, Poseidon, Archie's letter, RIVER. She drew a small feather at the end of the chain, in the margin. Not a note. Just his mark in the record. Archie looked at the scroll. Then at Athena. He looked at the feather in the margin. He did not slow-blink again. He just turned and looked out the window, at whatever direction east was.
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena sealing the letter — pausing — 'your letter worked' — Archie's slow blink — Athena looking at her scroll with the chain: Titan, river, expedition, Poseidon, Archie's letter, RIVER — drawing the small feather at the end of the chain in the margin — not a note, just his mark — Archie looking at the scroll, at the feather in the margin — not slow-blinking: this is different from the blink moments — looking east — the feather mark in the margin as the closing visual: Archie's contribution notated in the record, not as words but as himself — the lamp warm, the evening eastern light — #3D2B00 and warm lamp amber, the scroll with the chain and the feather mark the final visual

viral_hook: The unnamed snake chose RIVER. 'Because the river was there the whole time even though it was underground. And I was there the whole time too. This took me eleven tries.' SEVEN's addendum: 'The first seven tries were my fault.' Zeus wrote back on his small paper: 'RIVER: from your left-side neighbors. We are glad you are RIVER.'
highlight_note: RIVER's explanation: 'The river was there the whole time even though it was underground. And I was there the whole time too.' Zeus's crown straight for the full read. The wobbly handwriting shows it took eleven tries.
short_title: The Unnamed Snake Is RIVER Now and the Reason Is Perfect
rewatch_bait: Trace the chain Athena traces in scene 3: EP026 (Titan mentions the river) → EP029 (team finds the underground river) → EP031 (Archie's letter about deciding carefully) → EP034 (RIVER names itself). Four episodes to produce one name.
youtube_title: The unnamed snake from the postscripts chose its name. It's RIVER. The reason is exactly right. | Little Olympus S2 EP14
lesson: You were there the whole time. Sometimes what you're waiting to know about yourself has been true the whole time you were deciding. RIVER knew what the name would mean before it chose it — it just needed to be ready to choose.
arc_notes: RIVER arc resolved — the unnamed snake from EP031 becomes RIVER, directly connected to the underground river arc. Athena documents the chain in her scroll with the feather mark. Perseus's snake knowledge established — he's been paying attention to each personality. FIVE's solidarity moment is the quiet character note of the episode. Feather mark established as Archie's contribution notation in the official record. Zeus's small paper appears again — he keeps small papers for specific things. THREE's 'RIVER is a good name.' with nothing else is the funniest line in the episode.
